description: A blocking call runs on the global/compute ExecutionContext, starving the pool and stalling unrelated async work.
body: Failure mode: a synchronous JDBC query, Await, or Thread.sleep executes on the global compute ExecutionContext (sized to CPU cores), so blocked threads occupy the pool and other Futures/IO tasks cannot make progress, collapsing throughput under load. Detection signals: latency spikes and throughput drops under concurrency; thread dumps show compute threads parked in blocking calls; blocking IO scheduled on the global EC. Do not run blocking work on the compute pool. Safer fallback: move blocking calls to a dedicated bounded blocking ExecutionContext, wrap them in blocking { } (Future) or IO.blocking (Cats-Effect), and load-test to confirm the compute pool stays responsive.
